query: Current NWS forecast for Death Valley Furnace Creek daily high temperatures July 11-23 2026, and any heat ridge/heat dome building in second half of July 2026 for Death Valley California
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**NWS Furnace Creek forecast (July 11–23, 2026):**
- Official NWS highs run **112–120°F** across the window, peaking at **~120°F on July 16** (and ~119°F on July 15 and, per model guidance, July 22).
- Overnight lows: 97–99°F.
- **No Excessive Heat Warning or Heat Advisory** posted for Death Valley as of the July 13 issuance — this is near to modestly above the ~116–117°F July normal, not a record-threatening event.

**Heat ridge / heat dome (second half of July):**
- **Yes, a heat dome is confirmed** by NOAA's Climate Prediction Center. The CPC Probabilistic Hazards Outlook (valid July 20–26) explicitly cites *"persistent mid-level high pressure over the Four Corners… bringing the risk of extreme heat for much of the western, central, and southeastern CONUS throughout week-2."*
- CPC prognostic discussion notes a *"strong anomalous mid-level ridge with above normal 500-hPa height anomalies over the northwestern CONUS,"* slowly de-amplifying, with above-normal temps favored across most of the country.

**Key nuance:** The ridge's core anomaly is centered over the **northwestern CONUS / Great Basin / Four Corners**, not directly over Death Valley. So while a genuine regional heat dome is building/persisting in the second half of July, current guidance does **not** show Death Valley reaching its most extreme historical thresholds (125–130°F) during July 11–23.

The full structured data (with all daily values, sources, and data points) has been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.snippet: World record highest air temperature 134°F recorded at Furnace Creek July 10, 1913; summer highs often top 120°F, lows in the 90s.

key_facts: NWS (WFO Las Vegas) Furnace Creek forecast highs, July 13-19, 2026: ~115°F (Jul 13-14), 119°F (Jul 15), 120°F (Jul 16, peak in official window), 117°F (Jul 17), ~116-119°F (Jul 18-19).
No Excessive Heat Warning or Heat Advisory was posted for Death Valley as of the July 13, 2026 NWS issuance; local AFD called temps near to slightly above normal for mid-July.
Overnight lows at Furnace Creek forecast at 97-99°F through the near-term period.
Extended model guidance (Open-Meteo) for July 20-23: ~118°F (Jul 20), 115°F (Jul 21), 119°F (Jul 22), 112°F (Jul 23); rising to ~121°F by Jul 27.
Across the July 11-23 window, forecast/model highs cluster in the 112-120°F range; no day guided into extreme 125-130°F territory.

CPC Probabilistic Hazards Outlook (valid Jul 20-26, issued Jul 12) cites 'persistent mid-level high pressure over the Four Corners' bringing extreme heat risk for much of the western/central/southeastern CONUS in week 2 — confirming a heat dome in the second half of July.

CPC 6-10/8-14 day discussion describes a 'strong anomalous mid-level ridge with above normal 500-hPa height anomalies over the northwestern CONUS,' slowly de-amplifying, with above-normal temperatures favored across most of the CONUS.
The core of the ridge anomaly is centered over the northwestern CONUS/Great Basin/Four Corners rather than directly over Death Valley.
July normal highs at Furnace Creek are ~116-117°F, so the 2026 forecast is near to modestly above normal.
Death Valley world-record air temperature: 134°F at Furnace Creek, July 10, 1913; recent July heat waves (2024) approached ~130°F.
